body:
Hi Javier,

Following up on my order from last week — the Sumatra dark roast, 2 lbs whole bean. Still showing "processing" on my end and I haven't gotten a shipping confirmation. I need that thing locked in before Thursday.

Look, my whole morning runs on one cup out of a Moka pot before I even look at my phone. It's non-negotiable. I'm at Linden & Rye Tuesday through Saturday and my brain does not fire on all burners without it — I'm talking actual mise en place mistakes, and my chef does not tolerate mise en place mistakes. The bag I have now is basically dust and coffee memories.

Can you check on the order status and get me a tracking number? Happy to upgrade to expedited shipping if that's what it takes.

Thanks,
Daniel Rodriguez

Hi Daniel,

Thanks for reaching out and sorry for the delay — I pulled up your order (#CR-48821) and it looks like it got held at the warehouse due to a brief inventory discrepancy on that particular Sumatra lot. Good news: it shipped out this morning and you'll have a tracking email hitting your inbox within the hour.

Expected delivery is Wednesday, so you should be covered well before Thursday. No need to upgrade shipping — we're absorbing the delay on our end and I've also added a complimentary 4 oz taster of our new Colombian dark roast to your package. Figured you might want a backup option in the rotation.

If anything else comes up or the tracking doesn't land in your inbox by this afternoon, reply here and I'll get on it right away. We appreciate your business and hope the next cup is exactly what you need.

Best,
Javier Rodriguez
Customer Support — Crown Roast Coffee Co.

Hi Javier,

Quick one — the pre-shift reminders stopped pushing through on my end sometime last week. I rely on those pretty hard Tuesday through Saturday. My routine is to check tonight's covers, any special requests, and run through the prep list before I leave the apartment, so by the time I'm walking into Linden & Rye at 2:30 I already know what I'm looking at before the mise en place review.

Without the notification I missed a VIP allergy flag on Thursday. Caught it during the 3 PM briefing, not ideal. Margaux noticed. Enough said.

Can you look into what's going on with my account? Notification settings, scheduling rules, whatever it is. I need those back on before Tuesday.

Thanks,
Daniel Rodriguez

Hi Daniel,

Thanks for reaching out and sorry to hear about the disruption — missing an allergy flag is exactly the kind of thing we want to help you avoid.

I pulled up your account and it looks like a platform update on June 22nd reset notification preferences for a subset of users, and yours got caught in that. Your pre-shift reminders were configured correctly before the update, so this is fully on our end.

I've restored your original settings — reminders should fire Tuesday through Saturday at 12:30 PM local time, which I believe is what you had before. You'll get a test notification within the hour to confirm delivery. If anything looks off or the timing needs adjusting, just reply here and I'll get it sorted same day.

Appreciate your patience and hope the rest of the week runs smooth.

Best,
Javier Rodriguez
Customer Support

Javier,

Got the test notification — came through clean, timing looks right. 12:30 PM works perfectly, gives me enough runway to review everything before I head out for the 2:30 arrival.

I'll be honest, I did not handle the Thursday situation with my finest grace. But we move. As long as I've got eyes on covers and special requests before I'm walking in the door, I can keep that from happening again.

Appreciate you turning that around fast. Seriously.

Daniel

Hey Papi,

Quick one before the week gets away from me — did the Battery loop this morning, three miles, cold front came through so it was actually bearable out there. Charleston in June, you take what you get.

Was listening to that podcast I told you about, The Mise En Place Hour. They dropped a two-parter with a GM from a Savannah restaurant group — whole episode on restructuring kitchen costs post-COVID. Most of it was stuff Margaux already runs tight, but there was a section on supplier negotiation timing that I hadn't thought about. Sent you the link. Figured you'd appreciate it given everything you navigated back at the restaurant.

Wanted to call but it was barely 7am, so here we are.

— Daniel

Mijo,

Already got through the first half on my walk this morning — you were right, that bit about renegotiating produce contracts mid-season was sharp. We tried something like it years ago but never had the numbers to back it up the way she does. Different era, different tools.

Tell me more about this GM. Is she building something new or tightening up an existing operation? I'm always curious what's actually moving the needle in other markets right now.

Glad you're keeping those runs going. Your mother asks about you every Sunday without fail — call her this week. Don't make me relay messages like I'm the front desk.

— Papá

Papi,

I'll call Mamá, I promise — tell her hi in the meantime.

So the GM's name is Reyes, been with the group about four years. Came up through front-of-house, which is interesting — her whole angle on food cost is guest-experience-driven rather than pure margin math. She's not cutting quality to hit numbers, she's restructuring menu logic. There's a specific bit about simplifying the protein lineup without guests registering the change — fewer SKUs, tighter prep, way less waste. Honestly sounds like exactly the conversation Margaux and I have been circling for the fall menu.

Finished the second episode on Wednesday's loop. Strong finish. Worth the full two hours.

— D

Daniel,

That's the right instinct. A tight menu executed well will always beat a sprawling one that's half-controlled — when you're managing covers and a full brigade, simplicity isn't laziness, it's discipline. Sounds like this Reyes knows what she's doing.

The fall menu conversation with Margaux is exactly where you should be putting your energy. You've been at Linden & Rye long enough to have real standing in that room. Trust your read and don't undersell it.

Proud of you, mijo. Call your mother.

— Papá

Hey Javier,

Got a duplicate charge I need sorted — $58.00 hit my Visa ending in 4412 twice from Wednesday night at Prohibition. Me and my buddy Danny were in around 8:30, one tab, one card, should've been one charge. Checked the statement this morning over my coffee and there it was, sitting there like a line cook who forgot to clock out.

I'm in the industry so I get it — busy mid-week service, POS acts up, stuff happens. No drama. Just need the duplicate refunded before my statement closes Friday the 3rd. Happy to forward the bank screenshot if that helps move things along.

Thanks for getting to it,
Daniel Rodriguez

Hi Daniel,

Thanks for flagging this — and for being patient about it. I pulled up the transaction record for Wednesday the 25th and confirmed the duplicate authorization on the Visa ending in 4412. You're right, one tab, one charge is what should have posted. We had a connectivity hiccup with our processor that evening that caused a handful of double-hits before we caught it.

The refund of $58.00 has been submitted on our end. You should see it back within 3–5 business days, but most cards are posting it in 1–2 right now. You'll be well clear of your Friday close.

Appreciate you reaching out directly instead of going the dispute route — makes it a lot cleaner on our end. Hope you and your friend enjoyed the night and we'll see you both next Wednesday.

Best,
Javier Rodriguez
Guest Services | Prohibition Charleston

Hi Javier,

Following up on my ticket from last week — still haven't gotten the Gmail API access sorted and it's starting to become a real problem. I'm trying to pull reservation confirmation emails into a simple script I wrote so I can cross-check covers before service, and right now I'm doing it manually every afternoon before 2:30.

The OAuth flow keeps returning a 403 on the readonly scope even though I enabled it in the console. I've reauthorized twice, deleted and recreated credentials, nothing. Screenshot attached from this morning's attempt.

I know this probably sounds like user error and honestly it might be — I'm a sous chef, not a dev, so I apologize in advance if I'm missing something obvious. Just need this working before the weekend rush hits.

Thanks,
Daniel Rodriguez

Hi Daniel,

Thanks for the follow-up and for the screenshot — that actually made this a lot easier to diagnose. The 403 on the readonly scope is almost always a consent screen issue rather than a credentials issue. If your OAuth app is still in "Testing" status in the Google Cloud Console, only users you've explicitly added as test users will be able to authorize it.

Head to APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen → Test users and add the Gmail address you're authenticating with. After that, run through the OAuth flow one more time and you should clear the 403.

If you're still hitting the error after that, send me the exact scope string you're requesting and the client ID (not the secret) and I'll dig deeper. Happy to jump on a quick call too if that's easier.

Best,
Javier Rodriguez
Developer Support

Javier,

That was it. Thirty seconds, done. I've been staring at that 403 for six days — genuinely thought I was going to have to keep doing this by hand until the end of time. My girlfriend Willa has heard about this OAuth screen more than any person in her life should ever have to.

Script's running, pulling confirmed covers correctly, already saved me fifteen minutes of back-and-forth before last night's service. Small thing, big win.

Appreciate you sticking with it. If there's a feedback form or survey, send it over — you earned a good one.

Daniel

Hi Javier,

Following up on my ticket from last week — still no luck getting the Google Calendar API to sync my shared kitchen calendar with the events I'm pushing through the integration. It's authenticating fine (OAuth goes through clean), but the events just aren't showing up on the shared calendar, only on my personal one.

For context, I'm running this on a personal project to manage prep schedules and covers for my team at the restaurant — we go Tuesday through Saturday, and I need the shared calendar visible to about six people. Right now I'm manually copying everything over, which, between you and me, is not a vibe at 2:30 PM when I'm already doing mise en place review.

Can you confirm whether the issue is with my calendar ID target or the scope permissions I requested? I've got `calendar.events` but maybe I'm missing something for shared calendars specifically.

Thanks,
Daniel Rodriguez

Hi Daniel,

Thanks for the follow-up and for the extra context — that actually helps narrow this down quickly.

The issue is almost certainly your scope. For writing to a shared (secondary) calendar that isn't your primary, you'll need the `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar` scope, not just `calendar.events`. The `.events` scope is read/write for events but it restricts writes to calendars you own as primary. Shared or delegated calendars require the broader auth scope.

Also double-check the `calendarId` you're passing in the insert call — it should be the full email-style ID of the shared calendar (e.g., `teamcalendar@group.calendar.google.com`), not `primary`. You can grab that from Calendar Settings > [Calendar Name] > Integrate Calendar.

Re-authorize with the updated scope and try a test insert. Let me know what you get.

Best,
Javier Rodriguez
Google Workspace Support

Javier,

Yep — that was it. Swapped in the full `calendar` scope, re-did the OAuth flow, and events are landing on the shared calendar exactly where they should. Honestly I feel a little cooked for missing that, but I've had worse days in the weeds.

One small follow-up: is there a clean way to set the `colorId` on events programmatically by calendar, not just per-event? I want prep blocks in one color and covers/specials in another so my team can parse the calendar at a glance before the 3 PM briefing. I looked at the docs but it got a little circular.

Also — just want to flag the original ticket response didn't mention the shared calendar scope distinction at all, which is what had me stuck. Might be worth a note in your FAQ or the scope documentation. Would've saved me a few days of spinning.

Thanks again,
Daniel

Hi Daniel,

Glad that sorted it — and no shame, that scope distinction trips up a lot of developers, especially when auth is passing clean and the problem looks invisible.

On colorId: yes, you can set it per-event via the `colorId` field in the Event resource (integer 1–11, maps to the palette in the Calendar UI). There's no native way to set a default color by calendar through the API alone — calendar-level color is a UI setting. So your best bet is to assign `colorId` explicitly when you build each event object, keyed off your event type (e.g., prep block = 5 / sage, covers = 9 / blueberry — whatever reads cleanest for your crew).

I've also flagged your feedback on the scope documentation gap to our content team — genuinely useful note, thank you for that.

Hope the integration makes those pre-shift hours a little smoother. Good luck with service.

Best,
Javier Rodriguez
Google Workspace Support

Hey Javier,

Circling back on the WhatsApp Business API setup we talked about last week — still haven't gotten the webhook verification to go through on our end. We're trying to get automated order confirmations and staff schedule pings running before the weekend rush, and right now it's just timing out every time.

For context, we're running this for Linden & Rye — it's a restaurant here in Charleston. Tue through Sat we've got anywhere from 80 to 140 covers a night, and half my pre-shift time is spent chasing down confirmations that could just be automated. If I can get this sorted before Thursday I'm a happy man.

Can you check whether the phone number ID we submitted is fully approved? I'm starting to think the issue is on the Meta side, not ours.

Thanks,
Daniel Rodriguez
Sous Chef, Linden & Rye

Hi Daniel,

Thanks for the follow-up — good news, I was actually just looking at your account. The phone number ID is approved and active on our end, so you're right to suspect Meta's side. What usually causes that webhook timeout is a mismatch between the verify token in your dashboard and what your server is returning during the GET handshake. Double-check those match exactly, including case sensitivity.

If that's already aligned, the next thing to check is whether your server is responding with a 200 status within the five-second window Meta requires. Anything slower and it silently fails — no useful error, just a timeout like you're seeing.

I've flagged your account for priority review given the business-critical use case you described. You should be clear to retest within the hour. If it's still hanging after that, reply here and I'll escalate directly to the technical team.

Happy to jump on a quick call if easier — just say the word.

Best,
Javier Rodriguez
WhatsApp Business API Support

Hey Javier,

Circling back on this — haven't heard anything since I submitted the ticket last week and I'm starting to wonder if it fell into the void. The issue is still happening: my developer app keeps throwing a 403 on the playlist-modify-public scope even though it's listed as approved in the dashboard. Tried revoking and re-authorizing, no luck.

I get that things move slow on the backend but I'm trying to get this sorted before the weekend. We run a pretty tight ship at the restaurant Tuesday through Saturday and my brain's basically on autopilot by Friday night — not the time I want to be troubleshooting OAuth flows between courses.

Can you confirm whether the scope approval actually propagated or if there's something else I need to do on my end? App ID is in the original ticket.

Thanks,
Daniel Rodriguez

Hi Daniel,

Thanks for following up — sorry for the delay, I can confirm your ticket didn't fall through. I pulled it up and I can see what happened: the playlist-modify-public scope was approved but there's a 24–48 hour propagation window that isn't well-documented, and it looks like your re-authorization attempt happened right in the middle of that window, which would have locked in the old token state.

Good news is the scope should be fully active now. What I'd recommend: go into your Spotify developer dashboard, delete the existing token for your app, then run a clean OAuth flow to generate a fresh one. That should clear the 403 immediately.

If you're still seeing the error after that, reply here with the exact request headers and I'll escalate to the API team directly. Let me know how it goes.

Best,
Javier Rodriguez
Spotify Developer Support

Javier,

That did it — clean OAuth, fresh token, 200 all the way down. Honestly should've tried that before submitting the ticket but c'est la vie. The 24-hour propagation window thing is a gotcha, would be worth adding a note somewhere in the scope approval flow so the next person doesn't spin their wheels.

Appreciate you tracking it down. I'm running Monday mornings pretty light before the week spins up so I had time to actually sit with this — good timing.

All good on my end, feel free to close it out.

Thanks again,
Daniel

Hey Javier,

Quick one — the gasket on my Bialetti 3-cup finally gave out this morning. Thing just sprayed dark roast all over my stovetop at 6 AM, which is a great way to wake up if you're into that. I've had it maybe two years, used it every single day, so I get it. I just need to know if replacement gaskets are available through you guys or if I'm ordering from a third party.

Only coffee I make at home is one cup before anything else, and that cup is non-negotiable before a shift. Tuesday through Saturday I'm walking into a restaurant kitchen by 2:30 PM — I need to be human before that happens. So turnaround matters here.

Let me know what you've got.

Daniel Rodriguez

Hi Daniel,

Sorry to hear about the early-morning espresso explosion — not the alarm clock anyone asks for! Good news: we do carry OEM replacement gaskets for the Bialetti Moka Express 3-cup. Part number BL-GK3, food-safe silicone, fits your model exactly. They typically ship in 2–3 business days standard, or overnight if you need it faster.

If the aluminum filter plate also took any damage in the blowout, I'd recommend grabbing that too — it's BL-FP3 and they usually go together after extended daily use. I can bundle both with a small discount if you'd like.

Just confirm your shipping address and preferred speed and I'll get that order placed today.

Best,
Javier Rodriguez
Customer Support

Javier,

Yeah, let's do the bundle — gasket and filter plate both. Overnight shipping, please. I inspected the plate this morning and there's a hairline warp on one edge, so you called it.

Shipping address: 412 Rutledge Ave, Apt 2B, Charleston, SC 29403.

Honestly a two-year daily run on a forty-dollar pot is pretty solid. I've seen sheet pans at the restaurant not last that long and those things get abused way worse than my stovetop routine. Willa keeps telling me to get a proper espresso machine but there's something about the Moka pot that I'm not ready to give up.

Appreciate the fast help. Get me that order confirmation when you can.

Daniel

Hi Daniel,

Order confirmed — BL-GK3 and BL-FP3 bundled, 15% discount applied, overnight shipping. Confirmation number is BL-2026-88431 and you'll get a tracking email within the hour. Estimated delivery tomorrow before noon.

And I'm with you on the Moka pot — there's a tactile ritual to it that a push-button machine just doesn't replicate. Two years of daily use is honestly a badge of honor for that thing.

Your new gasket and plate will be ready before the next shift. Enjoy the cup.

Best,
Javier Rodriguez
Customer Support

Hey Javier,

Following up on the pre-shift reminder config we set up last week. Short version: it's mostly working, but Tuesday's notification came in at 1:47 PM — I'm already out the door by then, moka pot's cold, and I've got zero time to flag anything before I hit Linden & Rye at 2:30 for mise en place.

The whole point is I need that window to scan covers, catch any allergy or special-request notes, and run through the prep list *before* the 3:00 PM briefing with Margaux. Walking in blind is not a great look for a sous chef. Trust me, she notices.

Can we bump the trigger to noon, Tuesday through Saturday? That gives me a real read-through before I'm elbow-deep in stock reductions.

Appreciate it —
Daniel

Hi Daniel,

Thanks for the follow-up — totally understand, noon makes much more sense for your schedule. I've updated the reminder trigger to 12:00 PM local time, Tuesday through Saturday, effective immediately. You should see it fire correctly tomorrow.

I also dug into the Tuesday delay you mentioned. Looks like there was a brief queue backup on our end around that timeframe — it's been resolved, so you shouldn't see that lag again. If it slips past noon even once, reply directly to the reminder email and I'll flag it as a priority.

Good luck with tonight's service.

Best,
Javier Rodriguez
Client Support — Scheduling & Notifications

Hey Javier,

Following up on my ticket from last week — still getting the same dropout on Episode 212 of "Behind the Pass." Happens right around the 18-minute mark, every single time. I do a 3-mile loop around the Battery on Monday mornings and this is the third week in a row it's cut out on me exactly when the host gets into the ghost kitchen economics breakdown. Which is, not coincidentally, the only reason I downloaded it.

Already ran through the usual fixes: deleted and re-downloaded, cleared cache, confirmed my subscription is active through March. Not a signal issue either — same route every week, every other podcast loads fine.

Happy to send a screen recording if it helps. Just want to get it sorted before Monday rolls around again.

Thanks,
Daniel Rodriguez

Hi Daniel,

Thank you for the follow-up, and I'm sorry this has dragged across multiple runs for you. I pulled up your account and found the culprit — Episode 212 had a corrupted audio segment introduced during an encoding update we pushed last Tuesday. It affected a small batch of downloads in that window, and yours was in it.

I've pushed a re-encoded version directly to your library. Force-close the app and reopen it; the corrected file should replace the old one automatically. If it doesn't sync within a couple of hours, delete and re-download and it'll pull the clean copy this time.

For what it's worth, the ghost kitchen segment is genuinely one of the stronger episodes we've had this quarter — worth the wait. Let me know if the dropout persists and I'll escalate straight to the content team.

Best,
Javier Rodriguez
Listener Support

Javier,

Opened the app this morning before my run and the file had already updated overnight. Made it through the full episode on the Battery loop — no dropout, all 42 minutes. The ghost kitchen breakdown was exactly as good as advertised, which frankly puts it ahead of most things I've been promised recently.

Really appreciate you tracking down the root cause instead of just telling me to reinstall. The encoding explanation made total sense; I should've flagged it after week one instead of re-downloading three times like a line cook who keeps tasting a broken sauce and hoping it fixes itself.

All good now. Thanks again.

Daniel

Hey Javier,

Following up on my last message — still showing a $40 hold on my card from the Revelry reservation Wednesday night. I cancelled that through your app before we even headed out; Danny and I ended up walking in somewhere on upper King instead.

I get it, stuff slips through the system. But I'm a sous chef, not exactly rolling in discretionary income, and $40 is my grocery run for two days. Can you confirm the hold's been released or flag it for whoever handles that?

Appreciate it.
— Daniel Rodriguez

Hi Daniel,

Thanks for reaching out and sorry for the hassle. I can see the cancellation came through at 7:14 PM on Wednesday — that's well within our window, so the hold should have dropped automatically within 3–5 business days of the original transaction date.

Could you confirm the last four digits of the card and the email used at booking? I want to pull the full record and make sure nothing's stuck on our end before I tell you to just wait it out.

Thanks for your patience,
Javier Rodriguez
Charleston Eats Support

Javier,

Card ending 7714, booked under daniel.rodriguez@greenridertech.com. The original charge hit last Tuesday the 24th, so we're past the five-day window — that's why I'm back in your inbox.

For what it's worth the app's great, use it almost every Wednesday. Danny and I have a standing thing where we try a new spot each week and I usually scout through you guys. This is the first time anything's gone sideways. Emphasis on first — he'd never let me live it down if I blamed a restaurant app for ruining Beers with Danny.

Let me know what you find.
— Daniel

Hi Daniel,

Got it — I can see the hold flagged as pending release but it never fully processed on our payment processor's side. I've manually voided it now and pushed through a confirmation to the card network. You should see it cleared within 24–48 hours; sometimes sooner depending on your bank.

Sorry again for the extra step. I've also added a $10 credit to your account for the wait — won't cover the full inconvenience but hopefully helps toward the next Wednesday scouting mission.

Take care,
Javier Rodriguez
Charleston Eats Support
